name: Mexico City

palette.name: Flag Green
palette.rationale: National flag green anchors civic identity across the capital's public ceremonies and monuments.

palette.name: Nopal Red
palette.rationale: A red accent references the Mexican flag and the city-origin Tenochtitlan eagle-and-nopal emblem.

palette.name: Cantera Gold
palette.rationale: Editorial warm stone tone for historic-center facades, plazas, and late-afternoon light.

sources.title: Secretaría de Turismo de la Ciudad de México
sources.publisher: Gobierno de la Ciudad de México
sources.note: Used for official tourism and civic context.

sources.title: Símbolos Patrios
sources.note: Used to distinguish national-symbol colors from editorial city colors.

notes: Seed record; official colors come from national symbols visible in the capital, while the stone tone is editorial.
editorialSummary: Mexico City's starter palette pairs official national green and red with an editorial cantera gold for monumental stone, civic ceremony, and dense historic texture.
